top_strategic.title: Longitudinal tracking possible: 72 players with 5+ clips
top_strategic.evidence: 72 players have enough data for improvement tracking. Top: PickleBill(656), Chintan(186), Tmf(133), Coach_Block(131), Irenefuntila(89)
top_strategic.action: Build player improvement trajectory product — track skill changes over time

top_strategic.title: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
top_strategic.evidence: The camera sees these items but we detect no brands. ZERO detections: accessories, apparel, footwear, wristband. Near-zero: sunglasses(2). Each gap = a brand that could be paying for visibility data.
top_strategic.action: Add shoe/apparel/sunglasses brand detection examples to Gemini prompt — immediate revenue unlock

top_strategic.title: Gear Forensics — Reverse Brand Detection as a Service
top_strategic.evidence: Instead of us finding brands in clips, sell a SaaS to brands where THEY upload their own product footage, training videos, athlete clips, and we analyze them against the Courtana corpus. 'Your racket appears in 847 real-world matches. Here's what players do with it.' Brands get competitive benchmarking (vs JOOLA's 6965), injury pattern alerts, technique adoption tracking. Brand becomes data buyer AND contributor.

top_strategic.title: Endorsement intelligence: 194 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences
top_strategic.evidence: Top pairs: PickleBill-JOOLA(1083x), Chintan-JOOLA(293x), Tmf-JOOLA(285x), Coach_Block-JOOLA(233x), Bricker-JOOLA(168x)
top_strategic.action: Build 'brand ambassador identification' product for equipment companies

top_strategic.title: Social caption generation at 99.9% — ready to productize
top_strategic.evidence: Social media captions are generated for 4307/4310 clips. This is already a viable content-as-a-service product.
top_strategic.action: Create API endpoint for social caption generation — charge per clip

top_data.title: Viral shot: 'overhead' clips average 5.12 viral vs 'none' at 1
top_data.evidence: Shot viral averages: overhead=5.12, speed_up=4.27, reset=3.88, volley=3.51, dink=2.92, drive=2.87, serve=2.18, unknown=1.14, none=1
top_data.action: Prioritize highlight reels featuring high-viral shot types

top_data.title: Prompt fix: 5 badges consistently missed — add positive examples
top_data.evidence: Production awards these but DaaS misses them: 'rally master' (6 missed), 'new look' (5 missed), 'steady eddie' (4 missed), 'paddle tap celebration' (3 missed), 'fresh fit' (3 missed). These are real badges that our prompt doesn't detect.
top_data.action:
Add positive examples to Gemini prompt:
'When analyzing badges, make sure to check for these commonly missed badges:
  - rally master: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - new look: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - steady eddie: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - paddle tap celebration: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - fresh fit: Look for [describe visual pattern]

top_data.title: DaaS badge prediction: F1=0.99 (precision=0.99, recall=0.98)
top_data.evidence: Across 546 verified clips: 2096 correct predictions, 16 false positives, 41 missed badges. Overall F1=0.987.
top_data.action: Target recall improvement: add more badge examples to Gemini prompt

top_data.title: Signature moves = viral gold: 4280 clips with signatures avg 2.9 viral vs 3.9
top_data.evidence: 4280 clips have identified signature moves, averaging 2.9 viral vs 3.9 for clips without.
top_data.action: Auto-tag signature move clips as premium content

top_data.title: Net/venue branding inflates 7 brands — true share-of-court differs from raw counts
top_data.evidence: Brands with significant venue-only detections (not product use): JOOLA (554/6965 are venue), Nicol (6/14 are venue), PUSHIT (5/9 are venue), Onix (4/45 are venue), adidas (4/47 are venue). Raw counts overstate market share. Product-use only: JOOLA=6411, Nike=685, PICKLEBALL=235, Reolink=132, HEAD=123.
top_data.action: Report TRUE share-of-court (product-use only) to brand partners, not raw detection counts

all_findings.title: Viral formula: quality (7.39 vs 4.37) + watchability (6.78 vs 2.43)
all_findings.evidence: Top 100 viral clips: quality=7.39, watchability=6.78. Bottom 100: quality=4.37, watchability=2.43. Both metrics track strongly with viral potential.
all_findings.action: Use (quality + watchability) / 2 as quick viral prediction score

all_findings.title: Endorsement intelligence: 194 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences
all_findings.evidence: Top pairs: PickleBill-JOOLA(1083x), Chintan-JOOLA(293x), Tmf-JOOLA(285x), Coach_Block-JOOLA(233x), Bricker-JOOLA(168x)
all_findings.action: Build 'brand ambassador identification' product for equipment companies

all_findings.title: Video reveals 60 patterns metadata never saw
all_findings.evidence: Across 16 clips, Gemini video analysis surfaced 60 visual observations flagged as novel vs the text metadata. Sample: The camera is positioned high and angled down, providing a wide, static view of the pickleball court.; Chairs are set up along the right side of the court, suggesting a spectator or waiting area.; A digital display or scoreboard is visible in the upper right corner of the frame, showing text that is diffic
all_findings.action: Feed these patterns back into the metadata extraction prompt — they represent systematic blind spots in text-only analysis.

all_findings.title: Metadata quality drifts from video reality by 0.5 pts on average
all_findings.evidence: Across 16 clips, Gemini video-based quality ratings differ from metadata by avg -0.5. 5 clips have metadata OVERSTATING quality (>=2pt lie); 2 UNDERSTATING.
all_findings.action: Re-train or re-prompt the metadata quality signal — it's drifting from what humans actually see on screen.

all_findings.title: Rally length matters: long rallies are more viral (long=3.1 vs short=2.2)
all_findings.evidence: Avg rally: 15.6 shots. Long rallies (15+): 2192 clips, avg viral 3.1. Short (4-): 66 clips, avg viral 2.2.
all_findings.action: Use rally length as a quality signal in highlight curation

all_findings.title: Prompt fix: JOOLA appears in 397/546 verified clips — add disambiguation
all_findings.evidence: JOOLA dominates 73% of clips. Prompt should distinguish: paddle logo (real JOOLA use), net logo (JOOLA sponsors nets), and venue branding (JOOLA sponsors the facility).
all_findings.action:
Add to brand detection prompt:
'For JOOLA specifically, classify detection source:
  - paddle: JOOLA logo on paddle face = confirmed product use
  - net: JOOLA logo on net = venue sponsorship, NOT product use
  - venue: JOOLA on walls/banners = facility sponsorship
Only count paddle detections as brand_category: paddle'

all_findings.action: Create API endpoint for social caption generation — charge per clip

all_findings.title: Top content creators: PickleBill leads with 656 clips
all_findings.evidence:
PickleBill: 656 clips, viral=3.2, Steady Eddie
Chintan: 186 clips, viral=3.4, Dink Specialist
Tmf: 133 clips, viral=3.4, Steady Eddie
Coach_Block: 131 clips, viral=2.8, Steady Eddie
Irenefuntila: 89 clips, viral=3.6, Steady Eddie
all_findings.action: Create player spotlight feature for top contributors

all_findings.title: Viral arc: 'athletic' is 220.0x more likely in top clips (22.0% vs 0.0%)
all_findings.evidence: Story arc 'athletic' appears in 22.0% of top-viral clips but only 0.0% of bottom-viral clips.
all_findings.action: Prioritize 'athletic' clips in automated highlight reels

all_findings.title: Brand-skill split: PICKLEBALL KINGDOM users excel at kitchen (7.5), PSA users at power (6.29)
all_findings.evidence: Among brands with 10+ clips: PICKLEBALL KINGDOM has highest avg kitchen mastery (7.5), while PSA has highest power rating (6.29).
all_findings.action: Build brand-skill correlation report for brand partnerships

all_findings.title: Body language signals present in 4/16 clips — untapped signal
all_findings.evidence: Gemini video flagged body-language cues (celebrations, frustration, gestures) in 4 clips that metadata scores don't capture. These correlate with viral potential beyond shot mechanics.
all_findings.action: Add a 'body_language' detection pass to the extraction pipeline — these signals predict shareability.

all_findings.title: Worst predicted badges: paddle tap celebration (F1=0.967), hustler (F1=0.93)
all_findings.evidence: Lowest F1 badges: paddle tap celebration (F1=0.967), hustler (F1=0.93). These need prompt improvement.
all_findings.action: Add specific examples for these badge types in Gemini prompt

product_concept.name: EquipmentSpotter
product_concept.first_screen: Dashboard showing 4 unbranded equipment categories (shoes, paddles, bags, apparel) with side-by-side comparison: current detection accuracy (showing 5 missed badge types) vs. improved model after prompt engineering. Red flags highlight the 5 badge patterns that need positive examples. CTA: 'Upload verified clips to train detector.'
product_concept.why_powerful: Directly monetizes the sponsorship gap by automating brand detection in unbranded categories, while transparently showing the data fix that makes it work.

product_concept.name: CourtShare Analytics
product_concept.first_screen: Sponsor dashboard displaying corrected brand visibility: left side shows raw detection counts (7 brands inflated by net/venue branding), right side shows 'True Share-of-Court' after venue-blinding algorithm. Sponsorship value card shows corrected ROI for each of the 4 whitespace categories. CTA: 'Claim your undetected sponsorships.'
product_concept.why_powerful: Reveals hidden sponsorship value by distinguishing genuine brand presence from environmental interference, enabling sponsors to negotiate based on actual court visibility.

product_concept.name: BrandClarity Engine
product_concept.first_screen: Search interface where users query 'JOOLA' (or other ambiguous brands) and see: clip count (397/546), disambiguation toggle showing 'true JOOLA detections' vs. 'likely false positives' with visual examples. Below: the 4 unbranded equipment categories highlighted as next expansion targets. CTA: 'Refine detection for your brand.'
product_concept.why_powerful: Solves sponsor-critical disambiguation (JOOLA confusion inflates metrics) while simultaneously mapping the sponsorship whitespace for category expansion.
